  1. Pre-Trip Planning
  2. Restaurant Recommendations

Restaurant Recommendations

Dining in Florence, Naples & Puglia

food
restaurants
planning
Published

June 4, 2026

Overview

Italian dining is an experience to savor. Key tips: - Reservations: Essential for popular spots, especially dinner - Timing: Lunch 12:30-2:30 PM, Dinner 7:30-10 PM (Italians eat late) - Cover charge (coperto): €1-3 per person is normal - Service: Often slow by U.S. standards - this is intentional - Tipping: Round up or 5-10%, not 15-20%

Florence Restaurants

Traditional Tuscan

Trattoria Mario (San Lorenzo) - Lunch only, no reservations - Shared tables, cash only - Authentic, cheap (€12-15) - Arrive 11:45 AM or 1:45 PM

Trattoria Za Za (Near Mercato Centrale) - Touristy but good - Florentine steak (bistecca) - Reservations recommended

Buca Lapi (Near Duomo) - Historic (1880), in basement - Famous for bistecca alla fiorentina - €40-60 per person

Mid-Range Favorites

Il Santo Bevitore (Oltrarno) - Modern Tuscan - Excellent wine list - Reservations essential - €25-40

Osteria dell’Enoteca (Oltrarno) - Local favorite - Daily specials - Good value

###Must-Try Dishes

  • Bistecca alla Fiorentina - T-bone steak, rare, shared
  • Ribollita - Tuscan bean/bread soup
  • Pappardelle al cinghiale - Wide pasta with wild boar
  • Lampredotto - Street food: tripe sandwich

Gelato

Vivoli - Since 1930, no cones (cups only) Gelateria dei Neri - Near Santa Croce La Carraia - Local favorite, less touristy

Avoid: Gelato with neon colors or piled high (tourist traps)

Naples Restaurants

Pizza (Essential!)

Da Michele (Via Cesare Sersale) - Since 1870 - Only 2 pizzas: Marinara or Margherita - Cash only, expect lines - €5-7 per pizza

Sorbillo (Via dei Tribunali) - Famous, always crowded - More variety than Da Michele - Reservations possible for dinner - €7-12

50 Kalò (Piazza Sannazzaro) - Modern, upscale pizza - Reservation essential - €12-18

Seafood

Trattoria da Nennella (Quartieri Spagnoli) - Chaotic, loud, authentic - No menu - they tell you what’s available - €15-25

Zi Teresa (Lungomare) - Waterfront, fresh fish - Reservations essential - €30-50

Must-Try Dishes

  • Pizza Margherita - The original (invented here)
  • Spaghetti alle vongole - With clams
  • Fritto misto - Mixed fried seafood
  • Sfogliatella - Pastry (ricotta-filled)

Pastries & Coffee

Pasticceria Scaturchio - Sfogliatella Caffè Gambrinus - Historic café, elegant Pasticceria Attanasio - Best sfogliatella riccia

Puglia Restaurants

Status: Recommendations Pending

Many meals will likely be included in Puglia tour. This section will be updated with tour restaurant information.

Expected Puglia Specialties

Pasta: - Orecchiette - “Little ears” pasta - Orecchiette con cime di rapa (with turnip greens)

Proteins: - Bombette - Meat rolls - Fresh seafood (Adriatic coast) - Burrata - Creamy mozzarella (invented in Puglia)

Baked Goods: - Focaccia Barese - With tomatoes and olives - Panzerotti - Fried calzones - Pasticciotto - Custard-filled pastry

Wine: - Primitivo (red) - Negroamaro (red) - Verdeca (white)

Potential Restaurants (To Confirm)

Lecce: - Le Zie Trattoria - Alle Due Corti

Polignano a Mare: - Grotta Palazzese (dinner in sea cave - very expensive) - Pescaria (casual seafood)

General Italy Dining Tips

Meal Structure

Italian meals have courses: 1. Antipasto - Appetizer 2. Primo - First course (pasta, risotto, soup) 3. Secondo - Second course (meat or fish) 4. Contorno - Side vegetable (ordered separately) 5. Dolce - Dessert 6. Caffè - Espresso

You don’t have to order everything! Common to order: - Primo + contorno - OR Secondo + contorno - OR Antipasto + primo

Ordering

Cover Charge (Coperto): €1-3 per person, for bread and table service Service Charge: Sometimes included (servizio incluso) - check bill Water: Ask for “acqua naturale” (still) or “frizzante” (sparkling) Wine: House wine (vino della casa) often excellent and cheap

Tipping

  • Not expected like in U.S.
  • Round up bill or leave 5-10% for excellent service
  • Never 15-20%

Coffee Culture

Stand at the bar: Cheapest (€1-1.50 for espresso) Sit at table: 2-3x more expensive

Types: - Espresso (caffè) - Standard coffee - Cappuccino - Only acceptable before 11 AM - Caffè macchiato - Espresso with dash of milk - Caffè americano - Espresso with hot water (watered-down to Americans)

Never order: Cappuccino after a meal (Italians will judge)

Reservations

Booking Platforms

  • TheFork (La Forchetta) - Popular in Italy, good discounts
  • Google Maps - Many restaurants allow booking
  • Hotel concierge - Can call ahead
  • OpenTable - Some high-end restaurants

When to Reserve

  • Popular restaurants: 1-2 weeks ahead
  • Very famous (Da Michele, etc.): Walk-in, arrive early
  • Lunch: Often less crowded, easier to get table
  • Weekends: Always busier, reserve ahead

Budget Planning

Meal Costs (Per Person)

Meal Type Budget Mid-Range Splurge
Breakfast €5 (café) €8-12 (hotel) €15-20 (buffet)
Lunch €10-15 (pizza/panini) €20-30 (trattoria) €40+ (full meal)
Dinner €15-25 (trattoria) €30-50 (restaurant) €70+ (fine dining)

Daily Total: €30-60 per person (budget to mid-range)

Money-Saving Tips

  • Breakfast at café (cappuccino + cornetto = €3)
  • Lunch as main meal (cheaper than dinner)
  • Aperitivo hour (6-8 PM): Buy drink, get free appetizers
  • Mercato Centrale in Florence: Great cheap food
  • Pizza by the slice (pizza al taglio): €3-5
  • House wine instead of bottles
  • Stand at espresso bar vs. sit

Dietary Needs

Vegetarian

  • Easy in Italy (pasta, pizza, vegetables)
  • Say: “Sono vegetariano/a”

Vegan

  • Harder but possible
  • Say: “Sono vegano/a, senza latte e uova”
  • Many pastas naturally vegan

Gluten-Free

  • Common (celiac disease awareness high in Italy)
  • Say: “Sono celiaco/a”
  • Look for “senza glutine” on menus

Food Allergies

  • Take seriously in Italy
  • Write out allergy in Italian
  • Show to server

Restaurant Checklist

During Trip
